review_text: Good amenities, clean and tidy. Good views and quiet park

A very nice caravan and cabin park. Very quiet as we were there in the off season.
Caravan sites were huge and ranged from standard to onsuite and ocean front. The amenities were modern and very clean. First young families there is a lob
lovely pool, a bouncing pillow, a water park and mini golf. Staff were friendly.
Our site was excellent and not overcrowded.|It gave us easy access to Port Broughton and nearby towns and all they have to offer.|The Staff were very friendly and accommodating.|Great stay.
Peaceful location.
We enjoyed the peace and quiet and on the beachfront. Everyone was friendly and we bought some fresh King George whiting and extra large tasty crabs. The hotel has great meals also.
Well maintained park with clean amenities good camp kitchen.|Great location within easy walk to cafes. Lovely walks along Spencer Gulf.|Good size sites.

The location is wonderful, and we particularly enjoyed the swimming pool and the mini-golf course. The park is well-maintained and the staff are incredibly friendly.||The sunrises and sunsets were truly spectacular. Our Jack Russell also enjoyed the walks, especially the fenced-in dog park near the caravan park, off the beach. We also appreciated the local amenities, including the Friday night fish and chips at the bowling club, the variety of goods at the bakery, and the convenience of the nearby IGA supermarket.||Thank you for a great experience... We'll be back!

We were invited to a family holiday, each had our own cabin. Our cabin was so dirty, the floors hadn’t been swept, mopped or vacuumed. There was dog fur everywhere on the floor. Hair through the sheets, the walls hadn’t had a scrub in a long time. Our air conditioner hardly worked and our door was broken. The slide door wasn’t shutting properly so we let the front desk know and they sent maintenance. Maintenance did what they could but it still didn’t quite fix the problem, so we had hot dusty wind blowing through the door all week. The curtains were so dusty we had actual chunks of dust falling off onto the bed and into the kitchen sink from the bedroom and kitchen curtains. The dishes were incredibly dirty. The pool needed maintenance and the kiddy pool desperately needed a clean. The water splash play area was a great addition but having it surrounded by sharp small stones is ridiculous.

If I had children big enough to sleep in their own beds, I sure wouldn’t be putting them in the bunks in these cabins. The room is a closet with 3 teeny tiny beds. The steps of the ladder spin creating a dangerous situation for a child that might slip and fall from the spinning. This happened in both cabins, not just ours.

My partner saw a small child fall off of her bike and badly injured herself. He carried her to the office as she had told him that her parents had gone to the shops. One of the women in the office advised him to take her to the hospital and said they couldn’t help with first aid. He had already told her he didn’t know the young girl. So leaving the park with a small child that isn’t his is absolutely out of the question, but the staff member didn’t see the issue. That made me feel uncomfortable that the staff advised that and couldn’t help with first aid, so always be present if your child/children are playing on their bikes/scooters etc.

We stayed for the family and the memories of the kids spending time together. Would we visit again? Absolutely not.

Edit to add: In reference to the reply my review received, all very generic. Maintenance told us that our cabin needed a new door and new air conditioner which they have told the owners and no permission for replacement has been given. As for the parents being happy with how the situation was handled, I’m sure they weren’t made aware that their child was almost sent off of the grounds with an unknown man. Lucky my husband is a decent man and said no to taking the girl to the hospital.

You can write whatever generic responses you want, I know they’re just damage control for the poor review that you rightfully deserve.
Excellent park, kept neat and tidy. Staff friendly and camp sites had shade and plenty of room for our camper.|Easy walking distance to jetty, playground and pub/shops.

We had a truly wonderful stay at Port Broughton Tourist Park. The park was exceptionally clean and well maintained, creating a comfortable and relaxing environment. The staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and always willing to help, which made our experience even more enjoyable.||We especially appreciated the peaceful atmosphere—it was perfect for unwinding and taking in the coastal charm. The facilities were excellent and clearly very well cared for, which did not go unnoticed.||Overall, it was a fantastic experience. We would absolutely recommend this park to others and are already looking forward to our next visit!

We have stayed here the last 2 years in a powered site, and have thoroughly enjoyed our stay both times. The park is very family friendly, is in a great location, and very neat and tidy! ☺️ Definitely get your value for money!

As kayakers a beachfront, powered site at the southern end of the park is perfect for early-morning launches into the shallow estuary.  Our site was nestled right next to a sandy pathway to the water's edge and a strip of remnant scrub was home to turtle doves, willy wag-tails, magpies, honeyeaters and native wattle birds.  The sites are large with fine dolomite bases, and we had a patch of lawn behind ours.  It's also quiet  at that end as most visitors with children tend to prefer to stay near the swimming pooll and kiddies activities.  The office staff are friendly and professional.  My only criticism was allowing our neighbours to erect a four-metre inflatable fence to contain three yapping dogs, exasberated by the owners shouting at them to shut up.  Credit to the staff who keep the park neat and clean.
Very clean and organised.Beautiful Park and facilities.
It's so loud with screaming kids and barking dogs around Easter time
Awesome Park. Lots to do and see.|Great cabins, fun park for the kids, pool, BBQ's, TV & games room. Putt Put.|Park run by great helpfull people.

We had a good time. Highlights were walks and fish and chips on a bench on the lovely grass beach front at sunset.

Caravan park, dog friendly cabin, was good. It would have been better to have a screen door on the sliding door to let in some breezes.

The fenced yard was great for the dog.

Definitely recommend the park. Really friendly staff, grounds are well kept and there’s heaps for families

Awsome services 👌 along with a perfect setting 👌
Everything immaculately clean and tidy
Ten out of ten
Great park caters  for everyone
Awesome caravan park. Sorry I meant to do a 5 star but fumble fingers hit 4.

Had a grass site near the beach.
Amenities were very clean and walking distance to the town. It was a lovely  stay and the staff were helpful and friendly 🙂
Fantastic Park. Great amenities, clean...good views. Pool...large van sites.
Very nice. Big shout out to Stacey on Saturday who took our booking. Right on the beach and within walking distance to everywhere.
Just make sure to bring your bug spray as the flies here bite.

Great park - staff were very friendly and accommodating- heaps of space and different types of sites - we had beach front with pool behind on artificial turf- 1km walk to town, pub and jetty.  Caught whiting out back of caravan park.

Initial concerns of cabin rooms were fixed ! eg. Beds weren't made! And terrible septic smell (inside) from cabin 14.
Lovely location and rooms were nice and clean.

Een hele goede kamping met zeer goede kwaliteit kampeerplekken en de faciliteiten waren ook heel goed en schoon. Ik vind het top dat de wasmachines betaald kunnen worden online! No more wisselgeld nodig.

Beautiful scenery.  Relaxing and Clean. And the Jetty very Safe to fish and crabbing. With very clean toilets. And friendly locals that more than happy to share the place to somebody that respect and don't abused the resources..like example by making sure  to only get the reasonable sizes  of crabs and fish. No sign of Algae Bloom in surrounding area.

Stayed 10 nights in December, great location, amenities very clean and showers are roomy.
Great for families big sites with some shade swimming pool, waterpark and bouncing balloon.

We had an amazing stay at this campsite. Our powered waterfront site had a beautiful beach view.The bathrooms and showers were very clean, and the kitchen amenities were excellent and wellmaintained.
A relaxing and enjoyable experience. Highly recommend this campsite for anyone looking for a peaceful beachside getaway!
A wonderful place to stay.
Great location, clean facilities, kid friendly. 4 blocks easy walk to the town centre and pier. I had a pleasant and enjoyable stay.
Well maintained park with fun water slides, pool and kiddies pool.
What an amazing place. There is a pool and a water park so much fun what a place
The park itself was pleasant and the staff friendly,however, the accommodation was very tired and certainly not up to scratch.  Accommodation wise it’s 3 out of 10 for me.

Spacious park with all that we needed for an enjoyable stay with friends and dogs. Clean amenities, lots for kids to do at the park. The beach was great, right on our doorstep it was peaceful.
I would come back.
We had so much fun here. Definitely re booking for more holidays . Perfect location by the water and the kids had a lot for fun .
Great park.
Not the cheapest caravan park but for what it has to offer its worth it, perfect place for kids

Nice well maintained park, sites are spacious plenty of room for the awning and car. Toilets and showers are very well maintained.
Easy walking distance to the town centre.
It's big! But that vould be great if you want company and friends for the kids.loads of facilities and well designed. Not a bargain at $50 for a powered site on the beach side.

This is not a place to visit if you are coming to celebrate. There is nothing to do here except fishing. The room rent is too expensive plus they don't give you any food and no main kitchen here, sofa,beds are all dirty. TV is very small & old. If I could rate it in minus stars, I would do it.

We are Lilly and Skye travelling around Australia with mum and dad.
We love the Port Broughton Tourist Park been there lots of times, close to our home in Adelaide. Close to the beach. Lots of zoomies had on the beach. We can walk around the town and mum and dad always stop at the hotel for beers and sometimes we get to share their food. Yumm!

Follow us....next stop Port Augusta Gday Discovery Park.
Great place to stay, clean, and friendly customer service
Awesome accommodation, pet friendly pet enclosure. Our dog could go out for toilet on her own.
We had a pet friendly cabin with a nice front yard for the dog.

More improvements done on the park and it's looking good. Good place to camp
The second amenities block clothesline needs the peppercorn tree to be pruned so the clothes get sun.

We stayed on  a powered site one night only Lyn at reception was very welcoming and cheerful the caravan park is immaculately kept and very quiet they mainly cater for grey nomads not kids no tent camping or rooftop .
Clean, friendly and even let us extend our morning until 11am 😃
Stayed in caravan park

Highly Recommend ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
We had a wonderful stay here at Port Broughton Tourist Park a lovely, well-maintained park with generous-sized sites that made parking and setup a breeze. The location is spot on, just a short stroll into town and close to local attractions, shops, and cafés.
We enjoyed several walks along the beach and out to the jetty, perfect for unwinding and taking in the scenery after a day off touring. The park itself is peaceful and clean, with great amenities including clean facilities, camp kitchen and BBQ area.

The staff were friendly, welcoming, and more than happy to help with any questions. Bonus points for being pet-friendly too, our furry friend had a great time!
Would definitely recommend for a relaxing and convenient stay by the coast.

We love this caravan park, having stayed with a caravan & also in a cabin we were very impressed both times. The amenities were spotlessly clean as was the park including the fish cleaning facilities. There was just the right amount of text messages from the park confirming our booking as well as on our departure day. This is one great holiday park I recommend. I’ve never stayed in hot weather but I have no doubt mosquitoes will be horrendous on those warm summer evenings due to its location near the water.

A well setup park with all the things a good park needs to keep the kid-lets entertained for any period of time. Pads for all sized vans, good range of Cabins, pool and right near a safe swimming beach for the kids. A great destination for something a little different from the built up areas.

Pretty coastal town.  Great little spot.  Very friendly welcome.  Small beach with easy access. The ensuite powered sites are great. Water pressure and heat are out of the world.  IGA and pub are a close walk (8 minutes).  Dog friendly with a portion of the beach allocated an off leash area.   Unfortunately, not everyone picks up their pooches poo on the beach.  Facilities look well equipped with a communal kitchen and pool.

We stayed in the Harvest Cabins, I had number 21 and my brother had number 20, these cabins are in very poor condition.
The web site says they have been refurbished, but I'm sorry these have not.
There were many defects that I have informed the park management about, so hopefully these are rectified very soon, it knocked down and replaced.
For this stay, these cabins, and a bit with the weather, made the short break very hard to enjoy, I would not be booking these cabins again in the future and I would not recommend staying in them for the price they are at, luckily there are other cabins in the park.
There is an upside to the caravan park itself, with a lot for the kids to do enjoy.

Nice quiet town and well maintained park.
Easy walk into town that has everything a traveller needs.
Very pleasant staff in reception and grounds men.
Great park for RVs and caravans. Clean well kept amenities. Great location on the beachfront. Love it. We will be back.
Quiet .clean
Great family park
It has cabins and caravans and it's like a playground , so many amusements for them
This is a great caravan park and located on the water and close to town an easy walk.
It's only a small caravan park. Has a pool and play equipment for the kids but the beach isn't that great.

Be careful and check prices. We extended our stay by one day and were charged a higher price. When we noticed, the staff told us, that prices at the counter are higher than online and that they are not willing to refund the difference.
During our stay, there was construction work with heavy truck traffic just in front of the sites. The management did not offer any compensation for that, just a lazy sms that there would be trucks passing.
When heading for the front desk at 10:05a.m., we were told in an unfriendly manner that we are checking out too late.
In general, the front desk staff was unfriendly and unhelpful.
Seems like they are only after your money and not your contentment. We expect a lot more customer orientation.

The park itself is nice. Many sites are quite large with trees and synthetic grass. But we are so dissatisfied with the way, we were treated, that we will not go there again.
Lovely small town to visit. Beautiful old pub with delicious meals.

It's a nice enough caravan park, clean amenities, cool water park that's lots of fun, really would be a great improvement if the sites had grass, wouldn't feel like such a dust bowl. Staff in the office are friendly and helpful.

The play facilities were great. We liked the mini golf. But we do feel some concrete from the bathrooms could be useful. Lots of dirt everywhere constantly, hard to keep clean in a caravan. Also bathrooms are growing a lot of mould.

We were applaud with the service of the female manager, we complained about the children bombing in the pool and the adults sitting drinking around the pool, the adults were encouraging them to jump as near a possible. When complained it ended in a screaming match and lead us being evicted from the park. They gave us 20minutes to pack up and leave or they would contact the police. We had paid for 10 days and first they said we would not get a refund. We did receive the refund however it was very humiliating to be told to leave. I was accused off using bad language and taking pictures of the children. WHICH WAS NOT THE CASE AT ALL.. the thing is the is signage around the pool with rules and there response is we can not monitor everything. They have CCTV which is near where the sit in the office, I’m sure they can look at it from time to time. We are extremely disappointed with this female manager. I’m sure they have rules they need to follow to keep the Gday name. Maybe Gday’s need to look into them. We will never return to this park and will never recommend it either.
We have spoken to some of the locals in Port BROUGHTON they have said they have upset locals also…..very BAD MANAGEMENT
Lovely relaxing park. Double synthetic grass sites at water front. Bit dusty though

Pros: Nice cabins, lots of amenities and play areas for all ages, and a great pool. Location is excellent, on the beack and a 5 min walk to the middle if town.
Cons: Beds are on the hard end of the spectrum, no fan in the 2nd bedroom and it got pretty hot.
All round a great stay ❤️

The lady’s at the front desk really need to get better at their customer service very poor attitude towards people very unlikely to return great location poor service & respect from your front liners who are the face of your business.
Brilliant park, brilliant staff, brilliant view's, easy walk along the beach front to the hotel and shop's. 👍

We stayed in cabin 6 during the last week of summer school holidays. This cabin was located towards the front of the park in a row of 4, it was quieter area of the park and had a grassed area alongside, with an access point to the beach close by.                                        The staff were friendly and helpful. The only downside was the furniture and linen inside the cabin were a bit stained and worn out looking. All appliances were clean and in working order.                                                       The facilities of the park were well kept, plenty of outdoor bbq’s and seating areas. The pool was maintained and in good condition. Overall a great place to stay with kids.

Synthetic grass site was disgusting. Had to clean all the rubbish off it when we arrived. Clearly hadn’t been cleaned in a very long time, covered in stains & dirt. Site was also close to dump point & it absolutely stunk. Toilets also disgusting. Not a nice experience. Wouldn’t recommend at all.

Hold my beer…

“Some Staff” need some customer service training. That’s the impression when you call to book!

A Premium Powered Site near the beach = Dirt/Gravel site that blows up so much dust, you’ll be cleaning your gear for eternity or just replacing it. It’s horrendous!

Nothing can prepare you for the onslaught of mosquitoes at dusk! Bring a blow torch!!

You’ll be doing your washing daily with all the dust but the machines provided, have never been cleaned.

The toilets. I’ve seen public toilets cleaner at a music festival. I watched a gentleman clean the men’s. Dear oh dear. Wiping the sinks and relaxing the paper is all….they are disgusting!!

The Camp Kitchen. Putrid. The floors look like someone tapdanced while cleaning 100 live squid.

Did I mention all the dust? Oh yeah, when you book ask for a site with a grass patch or fake grass. I just sneezed mud…

What was good?

The pool, the waterpark, and having a shower when I got home and not booking for next year. Also sending a slightly more eloquent version of this review to the Head Office. This park has potential but cost cutting and blatant laziness is making it bad.

Excellent caravan park. Visitied 2/1/25-8/1/25. Has ALL the kids facilities and activities. The park is busy but not too busy/crowded, so we could use all the facilities in comfort and serenity. Local IGA and shops close. Definately recommend.

Good for caravan or cabin only. For tent, there are bad powered sites that facing toward the channel, which attract tons of mozzies, flies and wind gust. Wind can come and go suddendly. Due to wind gust, we had to cancel our trip. No credit or refund either due to bad weather.
Lovely spot, kids loved it too. So busy didn't take any pictures

Sorry to have to post a bad review, but for $60 per night powered sites,I would of thought this park could pay a decent cleaner. The amenities block has not had a good clean for years. Scum & mould build up in showers & dirt, dust, hair in corners of toilets.
Will not be going back 👎👎👎

This park is almost the heart of the community. The facilities are first class and the sites are very large. One of the best camp kitchens I've seen on our travels. Amenities are large and very clean as well. Fishing from the beach right in front of the park is also a huge bonus for onshore fishermen. You can almost catch you fish and have them in the pan before they stop flapping. We will definitely be going back in the very near future.

Well we did come back November 2024 and it is just as good as my last review plus the caravan, motorhome and tent sites are now covered by synthetic turf. This is a great improvement to ensure you are comfortable. As I said in my first review about yhe Park being a big part of the community this was highlighted on my recent visit in that the local Primary school community were conducting students learn to swim classes there. An excellent sharing of the recreational and educational resources available at Port Broughton.

Wow! What a great Tourist park! We have 11 and 13yr old children who loved the water park, shaded swimming pool, jumping pillow, mini golf and play equipment here! There was also a skate park, climbing equipment for older children near the pool and a laundry which we didn’t need to use (we have one in our van) during our stay. The facilities are well maintained and clean. The grounds tidy and well kept. The staff were welcoming and helpful when we asked any queries. We stayed on a powered site. Would definitely stay here again.

Does not cater for self contained travellers on a budget. No unpowered sites. $42 for a dirt site seems unreasonable when you can get a beautiful site elsewhere on the East coast for udner $35. Both van parks were under the same management.
We did find an RV park about 8km for $10 a night but inland from coast. Port Broughton is a pretty spot but overly regulated and therefore not welcoming to the average traveller.

Camp kitchen was hardly functional. Kids running around till 9 pm screaming. Laundry didn't work well, both driers never got hot and washing machine left clothes soggy wet.  Better money spent up the road, It was overpriced and neglected.
We absolutely love going here for family holidays. So close and convenient to everything.  And such a relaxing atmosphere for the whole family.
Nice place. Modern and clean 👌
Not a bad park, we had plenty of room, could choose our own sites, toilets and showers pretty good, excellent water pressure. Laundry a let down with busted machines
Well run. Very clean park. Great place to stay whilst visiting the area.
was as a great spot
Nice quiet site on the water, amenities were nice and clean, however the showers were lukewarm at best.  People deserve a nice hot shower after a long day travelling.

Stayed in spa cabin , sheets on double bed were dirty, no dustpan no tongs had to go to the office to get the things that were missing. I'm highly allergic to dust mites so I'm very particular with cleanliness and the cabin floor under the dining table was dirty,  spider webs around lights the kitchen TV was barely viewable with finger prints all over it .
The main room has 2 bedside lamps and a double electric blanket but 1 PowerPoint on the window side of the bed so no good for the other person and the electric blanket didn't even work put it on all day and didn't heat at all

Just spent a great week at the park , we used the ensuite sites which were spotless with astro turf next to the van .
If you're after lush grassy sites, sorry, it's all grey road base with astro turf. All the sites are immaculately clean and raked of leaves and there is no litter any where.
We don't have kids but there is a hell of a lot for them to do at this park.
Checked out amenity block and it was spotless and there are two blocks.
Ensuite sites are at the back near the main road but there isn't a lot of traffic so noise is minimal.
A really great park.
What a great place to stay pet friendly. Coco had a lovely time. We will be back
Clean tidy cabins great location.
Tidy Park

A death in the family isn't enough reason to cancel a booking and hope for a partial credit. Just made a crap day a little more crappy. Suffice to say i won't be looking this way again if it's all about the $$$ and not people. The office staff were lovely and tried to be accommodating but the manager overruled them...rules are rules right.
Great place for our first caravan trip. Parked next to the water, and our dogs loved walking on the beach. We will visit again.
Fabulous place to stay
Fantastic location and was lovely and quiet as it was off season. Staff are very friendly and helpful. The camp cat decided to live with us while we were there .. so lovely.

A very nice place to stay. Good areas for kids, good facilities and friendly staff.

The sites are huge in comparison to other parks and its proximity to the town is perfect.

I can't fault this park.

A nice, quiet park right on the waterfront, offering great sunset views and pleasant walks along the shore. There are a variety of waterfront sites and astroturf powered sites to choose from. The facilities are clean, and the showers are good. The resident kitty is a friendly little buddy, adding to the charm. Plus, it's just a short walk to the pub and supermarket, making it very convenient.
One of the best in the top end of the York Peninsula. We have been there many times over the years and also had a permanent van there for many years and also seen many changes and owners.
Beautiful spot

Great scenic park next to the ocean and well maintained. Many sites have synthetic grass surrounded by crushed rock and are level,there are some trees. Facilities include excellent toilets and showers, terrific camp kitchen, BBQ areas, laundry, pool, kids playground, mini golf, water slide, fish cleaning area and wonderful friendly staff. Town small.

Beautiful caravan park situated right on the water large shaded sites fantastic clean amentaties camp kitchen has everything needed included a pizza oven,quiet location at the end of the foreshore street within easy walking distance to the Main Street and pub with kid friendly bouncy castle and pool
The owners are exceptionally friendley and helpful just a great park to stay at
Amazing place, great new clean amenities, so much for the kids to do, friendly helpful staff. We will definitely be back

Stayed in a cabin previously and it was run down and not very clean. Stayed recently and booked a premium waterfront camp site, was expecting some grass but it was a dust bowl. Shower blocks power kept tripping all the time.
The activities for kids could not be faulted though. Splash pad, mini golf etc and pump track are fantastic.

Staff were friendly.
Cabin was dirty. Stains on the sheets which were absolutely disgusting. I asked for port cots. When I arrived we didn't get it. So was delivered straight away. Full of mice poo. Staff member cleaned it out in the cabin. Left mice poo on the floor. Cob webs in the corners. Windows dirty. Didnt have many rubbish bins around the place. Kids with bikes, scooters, etc riding around very fast not slowing down. Nearly ran into us a few times and had to jump out the road. Very dangerous.
A very nice place. Although very expensive. Won't be in a hurry to go back.
We visited friends staying there. Looked really cool. Lots for kids to do, super close to the water too.
Wonderful place, dog friendly

We love going here, been going for a few years now, plenty to keep kids happy with pool, jumping pillow, mini put put, skate track, playground and soon there will be a splash zone with slides! Management are friendly!

We stayed 14 - 18 March 2024.
I booked the 2 King George cabins (cabin no. 26 & 27) as I was taking my 2 adult daughters and their families.
On their website is says "**NEW** 3 Bedroom Waterfront Villa.
I've had these booked for over 12 months and it was our first family holiday all together.
I paid $2600 for both cabins for the 4 nights.
One of my daughters had stayed at the park with her family approx. 18 mths ago and we chose the park due to the facilities it had.
The communication with the staff was great. I asked about a bbq and they said these cabins don't come a bbq however they could arrange one.
On arrival there was no bbq, so I called the office and was told they font come with bbq's. I then told them I'd enquired and was told they would arrange one. They said they get one organised.
When the person delivered it, he told my son in law he'd taken it from another cabin, that was empty and it might need to be cleaned. I was surprised that they didn't check this and clean it.
Upon entry it was clear these are not **NEW** as advertised on the website.
The cabins were also not clean. Below is a list of what we found.

Cabin No. 27
The glass sliding door hadnt been cleaned.
Screen door had holes in the screen.
Range hood drops of oil and the filter was failing out.
Floor in the main living area, against the glass sliding door as really dirty.
One of the sheets in the bunk room is stained.
The living area ceiling corners have not been dusted, there were bugs stuck in cobwebs.
Top kitchen draw under the cutlery tray is filthy.
The dining chair are white and appeared dirty
The dish rack is dirty.
The main bathroom door had clear water damage.

Cabin No. 26
The glass sliding door was clean.
Screen door had holes in the screen
One bunk had no matress protector.
No pedestal fan in the bunk room, there is one in no. 27.
The clothes airer is rusty.
Range hood drops of oil and it's filter was failing out.
Some of the cutlery and plates were dirty and the cutlery tray is filthy and under the tray is dirty.
The living area ceiling corners have not been dusted.
The dish brush should have been replaced mths ago.
An empty lolly packet under the bed in the main bedroom.
The white dining chairs appeared dirty.
The stove top knob are dirty.
The main bathroom door had clear water damage.

All the windows in both cabins were really dirty.
We could not connect the TV'S or phones to the parks wifi.

I addressed this within a few hours of arrival and the front desk said they'd let the manager know. I asked if the towels would be replaced during our stay and the front desk advised they were going to be really busy over the weekend and they couldn't replace them as the would have enough towels, however I could wash them in the laundry. When I looked bewildered, she said I could drop them off and they vould wash them and bring them back.

The manager came to the cabin shortly after. He agreed what we'd experienced wasn't acceptable. G
He got the cleaners to come in and address the cleaning issues and offered all the children a free icecream.

As for the park itself, it's tired.
2 of the 4 washing machines, were out of order.
1 of the 2 pizza ovens, were out of order.
The water in the toddler pool had about 1/3 of the water level and on the 17 March it was filled up.
The grounds around our cabin had used party poppers, used balloons, and other small pieces of rubbish about.

Awesome park if you have kids! So much to do! Dump point, fish cleaning/ boat cleaning station, tidy sites and CLEAN bathrooms. We've been twice and will go back again. The waterpark is fun for all of us!
Great location to bring the kids. Putt putt. Water park and the sunsets are a bonus while going for a walk on the beach.
Weekend away with family.  Great place with lots of facilities  at a god price .
Great place for a  holiday,great pool and camp kitchen

Just had the best five days away in a brilliant caravan park. The staff were amazing and couldn’t have been more accommodating. Loved EVERYTHING about this park, you won’t be disappointed!👍🏻👍🏻

Another great 5 days, very clean ablution block, awesome boat/fish cleaning bay with crab cooking facilities. Other parks could learn from these guys!
Beautiful quiet place to relax and recover

#1, as usual wifi may as well not be there.  The daily limit is so miniscule that it is useless for anyone let alone an autistic teenager whose data wouldn't work in the area either.  Thank God we could hot spot my data to her phone or we would have been going home early.
Staff were lovely however there was no way for them to override the wifi system.  At other parks there's a code for extra wifi.  Here it's just 600kb per device per day.
They need to introduce a deposit system for golf sticks and balls because whenever we wanted to play there were none available because people simply don't return them.
Your pool gate needs repairing.  It stays open unless you purposely close it.
Pool is lovely and was a great temperature during our stay.
The water park,  playgrounds and bike track are excellent things to have.
It would be great if there was a plug per sink in the camp kitchen so you don't all have to wait to use one sink for dishes.  And the camp kitchen sliding door is incredibly difficult to open,  virtually impossible if you have an arm full of food supplies.
We found the other holiday makers to be delightful and we had a great holiday.
We will not return unless the wifi is fixed.
Also don't rely on the tourism wifi at the foreshore, it has been disabled.
Fantastic stay heaps for kids to do. Dog friendly.  Staff were very friendly and helpful.  Stayed here before and will be back. Highly recommend

Great park with plenty to do for the kids between the water park and the pool. Movies at night was a great feature during school holidays. Camp kitchen clean, plenty of amenities. Had no hot water the first day was here not sure if there was an issue as was hot the rest of our stay. Had to remind other park patrons to pick after their dogs on the lawn area so if u plan on bringing your pooch please pick up after them!!!
Great place to get away from it all.
Great water playground for kids up to 12-14
Was a good friendly . The onsuit was great all up great for kids and adults
Can't believe what I saw yesterday! I watched a man spraying weed killer POISON right next to where people eat and the children were running straight through where he had just sprayed!!

Due to a sudden heat wave spike and with two small children we needed to reschedule. I rang to get a credit, I was told this was fine. I rang back with my new dates and was informed " we made a mistake" and could not cancel due to short notice. I understand cancelation policies but to be told one minute it's fine and the next it's not, throws you a bit. The blame was turned around on myself and I was spoken to in a very rude manner. This was our favourite place to attend in summer and sadly we won't be back.

Our 5th visit to Port Broughton Caravan Park during School holidays in as many years! A great activities programme for children, combined with a fabulous, well shaded pool, huge waterpark and mini golf, and also a skate park...and the beach on our door step! It lacks absolutely nothing...not withstanding fishing off the jetty for Flathead and Blue Swimmers makes it a Paradise for all. The amenities within the park complex are well maintained, the staff are very approachable and friendly too. Our grassed site is better than most caravan park sites in that its large size, is second to none! We love it at this very family and pet friendly Caravan Park and highly recommend it to everyone too.
Great Staff

Great place to spend with family.... very affordable 👌  well kept grounds and the cabins are clean and welcoming 🙏 😀  the water is simple and fun with slides for kiddies... and the big adults kids  swimming pool... have a round of put put and the views are amazing great walk to the shops and the jetty close by 100% will visit again
Great location! Gorgeous sunsets! Great amenities! Highly recommend.

Love this tourist park and have been here several times.
Just a couple of things, dolphin cabin says it comes with Electric Blanket, which it doesn’t but it’s ok they provided warm blankets.
And the DVD player didn’t work so I couldn’t watch movies and Wifi wouldn’t connect. Aside from those little things this place is fantastic and everything is in walking distance. Aircon-Heating worked super well.
What a great holiday destination, loving it 🙂

I recently stayed there in the school holidays with my daughter & grandkids for a week (too long). We had a spa cabin however the spa didn’t work, had to ask reception on 2 separate days to get someone to have a look at it then be told it’s not working. No reimbursement at all, could’ve just booked a normal cabin. No robe in main bedroom, needs a ceiling fan as air cond only really does main living area. Mattresses on bunk beds are thin as, not comfortable at all. No extra blankets at for family of 5, pillow covers need replacing as they were yellow. Thankful that I brought my own pillow. We had no matches for gas oven. Some oven dishes would be good so that we could cook in the oven. Gas ran out on Friday night, rang after hrs & they did fix it temporarily until can get more cylinders. More kids activities would be good. Paying $10 for face painting is just ridiculous!! Maybe you need to look at other parks for ideas. If I were to recommend this park, I would say to only stay for few days as that’s enough.
Clean tidy modern
Just look at the photo. This park has everything you need!

So I will be balanced.  We had a premium cabin with absolute beach front.  It was an amazing location.  But right up service and rooms were a little less than premium.  Booked a cot but informed all had been used.  Not ideal but it was sorted quickly.  In the rooms not enough crockery for the people 3 mugs 1 wine glass, a premium  spot probably needs more than 1 wine glass.  No quilts on the bed and there were cols nights.  Just a thin bed cover.  One blanket in the whole place that sleeps five?  Place was advertised as having a queen bed.  I have a queen bed at hone  this is not a queen bed in the main  room.  Free WiFi advertised which was good as my phone carrier didn't work.  Except it didn't reach these cabins, when I asked it was " that's just the way it is"  again premium price not premium service imo.  Overall it was not bad just not what you expect for the money.  Wouldn't avoid it but can't recommend it either

This park has the complete package! Best we've been in. Caters for everyone. Kids facilities include playground that's 2nd to none, amazingly set out. Pool is very clean as is everything in this spacious park. Staff are well informed and super helpful.  I can't recommend this park highly enough. The vista is so relaxing. There is plenty of waterfront to enjoy and miles of beach to exercise your pets. Perfect!!
Beautiful location  great clean cabins and park friendly customer service
Great facilities
Excellent. Brilliant. Has everything. Good deals. Good Pub near too.
Great Cabin Great Location everything was spotless very friendly staff
Incredibly kid friendly, great staff at reception and the views make it an absolute gem. Fire pits for $30 made the night time experience memorable 🥰
Great location. Waterfront sites.  Very spacious grass sites with lots of trees.  Clean amenities.  Pet friendly.
Great family place, kids would have a great time, and 🐕 friendly. Fantastic staff and well looked after
Great Park, awesome friendly staff, lovely cabins.
The room was clean, my only complaint is that he mattresses on bunk beds far too thin and even with two it was still very hard to sleep on

Neat clean
Friendly staff
